title: feat(filesystem): add prefix scoping and STS session token support for S3/GCS mounts

similarOrDuplicatePRs.title: feat(s3): add STS sessionToken support for temporary credentials

summary: Adds two features to S3 and GCS FUSE mounts in E2B sandboxes: (1) a `prefix` option that scopes mounts to a subdirectory using s3fs `bucket:/prefix` syntax and gcsfuse `--only-dir` flag, and (2) `sessionToken` support for STS/AssumeRole temporary AWS credentials via AWS credentials INI file. Includes a `validatePrefix()` function to guard against shell injection, and exposes prefix from `getMountConfig()` in both S3Filesystem and GCSFilesystem. Changes span 7 files across workspaces/e2b, workspaces/s3, and workspaces/gcs.
maintainerNotes: Well-structured community contribution with tests covering prefix inclusion, trailing-slash stripping, and omission. CodeRabbit and Copilot reviews flagged 3 actionable issues: (1) SAFE_PREFIX regex rejects single-char prefixes, (2) prefix='/' normalizes to empty string but still gets set on config, (3) same empty-string edge case in S3. These have NOT been addressed by the author yet. No changeset included — author needs to add one before merge. CI test suites (Core, E2E, MCP, Memory, RAG, Combined stores) are all pending — likely because external contributor PRs don't trigger secret-gated workflows. Author has an earlier draft PR #13247 covering just the STS session token part; this PR appears to supersede it — #13247 should be closed if this merges. Mergeable state is UNKNOWN (may need rebase).
